title: Acrolein + PFA Perfusion for Immunocytochemistry
author: Cleyde V Helena
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tb9eir6

steps.instruction: Prepare animal as for Paraformaldehyde fixation; e.g. animal anesthetized with Nembutal, open chest cavity; inject 0.1 cc heparin into the heart apex before first rinse. We clamp the descending aorta to eliminate whole body perfusion. Cut atrium.
Saline nitrite rinse until clear effluent; about 150-200 ml/225-250 g rat. undetermined, 0.9% saline undetermined, Sodium Nitrite

Switch to Acrolein/Paraformaldehyde; continue until animal is hard, as judged by the front feet. Requires approximately 150-200 ml/225-250 g rat. undetermined, 8% PFA undetermined, 0.5 M Monobasic Potassium Phosphate undetermined, 0.5M Dibasic Potassium Phosphate undetermined, destilled water undetermined, acrolein add acrolein just before ready to perfuse; if using a sealed bottle or flask, slowly release pressure after doing so and before perfusing
Second saline nitrite rinse; ≈150 ml. No post-fixation necessary.
